subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: The delivery temperature of heated water at the outlet of each sanitary fixture for a new heated water installation, or for an existing heated water installation if the requirements in this clause applied to the installation when it was a new installation, must be—

ol.li:
not more than 45°C in any—
residential part of an aged care building; or
patient care area in a health-care building; or
part of an early childhood centre, or primary or secondary school, that is used by children; or
designated accessible facility in a common area of a Class 2 building, or in any part of a Class 3, or Class 5 to Class 10 building; or
not more than 50°C in all other cases.

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: The delivery temperature of heated water at the outlet of each sanitary fixture (other than a basin in a Class 1, 2, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8 or 10 building) for an existing heated water installation must meet the requirements for a new heated water installation if—

ol.li: in relation to a heated water installation with multiple water heaters — all water heaters in the installation are replaced or relocated; or
in any other case — a water heater in the installation is replaced or relocated or an additional water heater is added to the installation.

callout.p: B2D5 applies to heated water installations intended for personal hygiene as stated in the clause.
